soap:
REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7572 — test(bitpacking): actually exercise u64 unchecked round-trip for widths 1..=63

LuciferYang (core) · XS (+4/-0, 1 file)

ONE-LINER
  Restore missing random-value fill for bit widths 1..=63 in fast-path bitpacking test, enabling 63 previously-silent monomorphized kernels to actually exercise non-zero data.

FINDINGS
  • rust/compression/bitpacking/src/lib.rs:2131 — The added `else` branch correctly masks random values with `(1 << bit_width)` to stay within valid range for the target bit width. Matches pattern from sibling `pack_unpack_u64` helper (lines 1923-1927). Test now covers the 63 fast-path monomorphized pack_64_N/unpack_64_N kernels that were previously exercised only with zero data.
  • PR body traces the origin: copy-paste omission during migration from upstream fastlanes (#2886, 2024-09). Upstream uses a different test skeleton that doesn't have this shape.
  • Author verified: cargo test passes (11/11), fmt/clippy clean. Only test file touched; no production code risk.

ASSESSMENT
  risk: low · urgency: low — Test-only fix that enables detection of bugs in existing fast-path kernels. No production code changes, no API surface, no behavioral impact. Fix is minimal (4 lines) and follows an established pattern. Risk is only whether the fix itself is correct — straightforward masking with no edge cases.
  escalation: none
  could not read: none

DRAFT COMMENTS
  (None — the change is sound and proportionate.)

RECOMMENDATION: approve
  confidence: high · what would make this wrong: if the mask logic were wrong for some edge bit-width (but (1 << bit_width) is unambiguous), or if the test itself were unsound (but it mirrors the existing working slow-path test).
summary: Test-only fix: restore missing else branch in unchecked_pack_unpack_u64 to exercise u64 fast-path kernels with non-zero data for widths 1..=63. Minimal change (4 lines), mirrors established pattern, author verified tests pass. No production code risk.
